perturbation.original_text: 4.1. Either party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days written notice if the other materially breaches this Agreement; provided, however that the party in breach shall have the right to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice of the other party's intention to terminate.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1. Either party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days written notice if the other materially breaches this Agreement; provided, however that the party in breach shall have the right to cure such breach within ten (10) days after receipt of written notice of the other party's intention to terminate.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the cure period for a material breach of contract. Originally, the breaching party had thirty (30) days to cure the breach, but now they only have ten (10) days. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the allowed cure period, causing uncertainty about the termination process. This directly contradicts the original cure timeline, and can cause confusion during enforcement as it is unclear on which condition should be prevailed when dealing with termination issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days written notice if the other materially breaches this Agreement; provided, however that the party in breach shall have the right to cure such breach within ten (10) days after receipt of written notice of the other party's intention to terminate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the cure period from thirty (30) days to ten (10) days. The contradicted text also states the cure period is ten (10) days. The original text says the cure period is thirty (30) days, creating a contradiction. By changing the cure period in Section 4.1, the contract now has two different cure periods defined which directly break compatibility with the original text. This directly contradicts the original cure timeline, and can cause confusion during enforcement as it is unclear on which condition should be prevailed when dealing with termination issues. Therefore it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.6 This Agreement contains the complete and exclusive understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. No waiver, alteration or modification of any of the provisions hereof will be binding unless in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of the party to be bound. Neither the course of conduct between the parties nor trade usage will act to modify or alter the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.6 This Agreement contains the complete and exclusive understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. A waiver, alteration or modification of any of the provisions hereof can be binding even if not in writing, based on the course of conduct between the parties. Neither the course of conduct between the parties nor trade usage will act to modify or alter the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that a waiver, alteration, or modification of the agreement can be binding even if not in writing, based on the course of conduct between the parties. However, it contradicts the original statement that 'No waiver, alteration or modification of any of the provisions hereof will be binding unless in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of the party to be bound'. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of modifications to the agreement. This in-text contradiction can cause confusion during enforcement as it is unclear on which condition should be prevailed when dealing with any modifications/alterations on the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the complete and exclusive understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. A waiver, alteration or modification of any of the provisions hereof can be binding even if not in writing, based on the course of conduct between the parties. Neither the course of conduct between the parties nor trade usage will act to modify or alter the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It initially states that the agreement contains the complete and exclusive understanding, and that modifications must be in writing. However, it then introduces a conflicting statement: 'A waiver, alteration or modification of any of the provisions hereof can be binding even if not in writing, based on the course of conduct between the parties.' This creates a direct contradiction regarding the enforceability of modifications not in writing, directly conflicting with the initial statement requiring written modifications and thus causes an in-text contradiction as described in 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
